operator_scope.semantic_signature: materialized layout transform or transpose/permute copy after correctness passes

valid_when: The task is a materialized transpose, permute copy, strided copy, or layout transform and a stride-correct candidate already passes hidden correctness.
Profile summary indicates memory-bound or uncoalesced global-memory access.
invalid_when: Task is view-only metadata transform, not a materialized copy.
Shared-memory use would exceed resource limits or correctness requires no reordering beyond the declared copy.
The dominant operation is gather/scatter with arbitrary indices rather than a regular layout transform.
preconditions: Correctness must be established with non-contiguous and edge-size cases before trying tiling.

mechanism: Regular layout transforms can trade one uncoalesced side for a tiled schedule that makes at least one global memory direction coalesced; shared-memory tiles may help but can introduce bank conflicts or occupancy limits.

instructions: Use a 2D or ND tile schedule that explicitly maps logical source and destination coordinates; keep edge masks for partial tiles.
Prefer coalesced reads or coalesced writes according to the larger contiguous dimension and benchmark both when cheap.
Consider a shared-memory tile only after the direct stride-aware copy is correct, and pad or adjust tile layout if bank conflicts appear in profile.
Tune a small tile set rather than hardcoding visible shapes; keep tile sizes independent of hidden benchmark shapes.
Do not call torch.transpose, torch.permute, contiguous(), or reshape() to perform the target copy in the submitted path.

expected_effect.correctness: Should preserve layout semantics if stride-index capsule is followed.
expected_effect.performance: Candidate-only: may improve regular layout-copy coalescing; no H20 speedup is asserted.
expected_effect.search_efficiency: Provides a bounded schedule search for transpose/permute copy tasks.
expected_effect.risk: Shared-memory bank conflicts, extra synchronization, or integer address overhead can erase gains.

performance_requirements: Compare direct stride-aware copy, coalesced-read tile, and coalesced-write tile under identical benchmark protocol before promotion.
validation.promotion_policy: Requires H20 benchmark/profile evidence and ideally an ablation against direct copy.

known_failures: Tiling can produce wrong transposed coordinates when source and destination strides are conflated.
Partial tiles without masks can read/write out of bounds.
Shared-memory tiles can introduce bank conflicts or reduce occupancy.
boundary_tests: permute with more than two dimensions
small dimension less than tile size

replace_on_new_backend: tile sizes, shared-memory layout, bank-conflict model
